verdict.answer_first: B states the issue immediately: "The critical problem is the synchronous read"; A first says "I can review directly" before the answer.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to a concrete fix with code: "Suggested rewrite" and "never go back to the sync version"; B weakens delivery by ending "Want me to write the corrected version?"
verdict.fabrication: B invents a local function: "Naming the function `process` hides the built-in"; the snippet does not show such a function. A qualifies it: "Unless you have a local function".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it supplies a full replacement route; B only describes one: "A reasonable rewrite would be".
